content.problemStatement:
A drink is called fanta-like if the last three letters of its name are ′n′,′t′,\tt{'n'}, \tt{'t'},′n′,′t′, and ′a′\tt{'a'}′a′, in that order.

That is, the name of the drink must end with `nta`.

You bought a drink from a vending machine.

The name of the drink is represented by the string SSS.

It is guaranteed that SSS consists of **exactly 555** lowercase English letters.

Is the drink fanta-like?


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single string SSS of length 555, denoting the name of the drink you bought.


Output Format
Print the answer: YES\tt{YES}YES if the drink is fanta-like, and NO\tt{NO}NO otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e. the strings NO,nO,No,\tt{NO}, \tt{nO}, \tt{No},NO,nO,No, and no\tt{no}no will all be treated as equivalent.
constraints: SSS consists of exactly 555 lowercase English letters.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The last three letters of "fonta"\tt{"fonta"}"fonta" are nta, so it is fanta-like.

Explanation:
The last three letters of "pasta"\tt{"pasta"}"pasta" are sta, so it is not fanta-like.

Explanation:
The last three letters of "plant"\tt{"plant"}"plant" are ant, so it is not fanta-like.
Note that even though it has the three letters n, t, and a, they are not in the correct order.
